Hero Hook: French illustrator in Annecy: bold flat color, minimal figures, childhood & silence—digital for global brands, acrylic for personal canvases.
Active Since: Graphic studio founded 2006 (Lyon, per LM); full-time illustration with Creasenso ~10 years as of Mar 2022; Emile-Cohl (Lyon) arts appliqués
Story (Full Text):
Jérôme Masi is a freelance art director and illustrator based in Annecy, France, represented by Creasenso. In a 2022 LM magazine interview he traced his path from École Emile-Cohl in Lyon into video games, then founding a graphic studio in Lyon in 2006, moving through a collective and motion design before committing full-time to illustration.

Commercial work stays largely digital for clients he names in interview—such as Orange, Tissot, and British Airways—while personal pieces often return to acrylic on canvas in a dedicated studio area, moving between desk and easel.

He describes his own taste as sober and minimal; the cover work “Childhood” embodies silence, interiority, and childhood, and he wants viewers to fill the intentional gaps in the image themselves.

Street Collector fits artists whose graphic language reads clearly at lamp scale: his flat color, strong silhouettes, and restrained palettes translate well to a small, lit surface without losing identity.
Pull Quote: « J'ai envie que chaque personne remplisse à sa façon les vides, les questions laissées sans réponse dans cette image. » — Jérôme Masi, LM magazine, 2022
Exhibitions (Text List):
2022 — Group exhibition, Taiwan (stated March 2022 interview)
2022 — Exhibition beginning April, Hangzhou, China (stated March 2022 interview)
Press (Text + Links):

Hero Hook: Cologne illustrator: flat, saturated vector scenes; broke out via “One Icon a Day,” Adobe MAX 2016, and Adobe Stock—bold work built for screens and products.
Active Since: Freelance illustration from 2016 (Adobe blog); prior study Mediendesign Rheinische Fachhochschule Köln
Story (Full Text):
Moritz Adam Schmitt is a Cologne-based illustrator and digital artist. Adobe’s 2017 “New Face” feature documents his self-taught push into illustration via the year-long “One Icon a Day” Instagram project, rapid audience growth, invitation to Adobe MAX 2016, and freelance practice from early 2016.

His interview emphasizes research, reference gathering, iPad roughs in Photoshop Sketch, then vector refinement—rooted in Adobe Creative Cloud workflows.

Influences cited in the same piece include street and pop art sensibilities and peers on Behance and Instagram; commercially he is linked to bold flat color and accessible iconography.

Street Collector: high-contrast flat illustration tends to read instantly on a small lamp panel, aligning with the brand’s need for legible, collectible graphics.
Pull Quote: „Ich lasse mich gerne inspirieren, stöbere auf Behance oder auf den Kanälen anderer Künstler. Die Community ist ein Sammelbecken kreativer Ideen und Impressionen.“ — Moritz Adam Schmitt, Adobe Blog, 2017
Press (Text + Links): Adobe Blog (DE) — 2017 — “Adobe Stock New Face: Illustrator Moritz Adam Schmitt” — https://blog.adobe.com/de/publish/2017/12/07/new-face-illustrator-moritz-adam-schmitt

Hero Hook: Bucharest illustrator & animator: dreamy figurative work—digital and paint—framed as “frozen moments” for editorial and global brands.
Story (Full Text):
Loreta Isac is a Romanian illustrator and animator based in Bucharest; The re:art (2020) notes she studied arts in Iași and introduces her practice with the line “once upon a dreamer.”

Her site and industry profiles present work across illustration, animation, and motion; Behance lists representation via Agent Pekka and membership in COLOR studio Bucharest, with client names including Apple, The New York Times, and The Washington Post (verify current roster on official channels).

She describes her journal-based practice as collecting “frozen moments” and draws from people, nature, events, and hard-to-name emotions.

Street Collector: her figurative, narrative illustration style gives collectors emotionally readable scenes that still work when scaled to lamp inserts.
Pull Quote: "It is like collecting frozen moments, a reality that you have to feel in order to understand." — Loreta Isac (via The re:art, 2020)
Press (Text + Links): The re:art — 2020 — “Once upon a dreamer: Loreta Isac” — https://thereart.ro/loreta-isac-works
Notes: Client list (Apple, NYT, etc.) appears on Behance; re-confirm on loretaisac.com or Agent Pekka before marketing claims. IG image URLs not auto-filled.
